rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M SAGONOWSKY VS. CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Protective Order And Request For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff Christina Sagonowsky
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 8, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN, PHIL FOSTER, ABIGAIL MORRIS, PAUL TOUR-SARKISSIAN, TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES' Motion For Protective Order And Request For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff Christina Sagonowsky. The motion for protective order and sanctions is granted. The court adopts Defendant's suggestion that Plaintiff select one set of special interrogatories, one set of requests for admissions, one set of document requests, and one set of form interrogatories for response. Plaintiff to make that selection by September 10, 2015 and Defendant shall respond by October 13, 2015. Plaintiff to remit $373.90 in sanctions by September 30, 2015. The court will not provide a reporter for this discovery matter. If the parties desire a reporter, then they must meet and confer and agree upon one. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: HEIDI DENTON et al VS. KWAN SHIU YAN FUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Provide Supplemental Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Production Of Documents, And Related Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 8, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT KWUN FUNG'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Provide Supplemental Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Production Of Documents, And Related Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The issues raised by this motion have been previously decided and are reflected in the Court's Interim Discovery Order dated July 27, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GILBERT PAPAZIAN VS. BLEY & BLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel Pursuant To C.C.P. ? 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 8, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT BLEY & BLEY, SAMANTHA DEJEAN, STEPHEN BLEY Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel Pursuant To C.C.P. ? 128.7. Defendants' Motion to Strike is GRANTED. Defendants' Motion for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is stricken as it is barred by the statute of limitations. (See Code Civ. Pro. § 340.6, subd.(a).) Plaintiff fails to set forth a cognizable basis for tolling. Sanctions in the amount of $4,653 are awarded. Defendants to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MARY RYAN VS. SAFEWAY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 8, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. First, Defendant's employee (Ms. Deng) cleaned up a mess at aisle 2 at 11:48 a.m. and a reasonable trier of fact could conclude that her failure to adequately clean the mess caused Plaintiff's injury. (Deng depo, 42:16 - 44:7). Second, there is a triable issue of material fact whether defendant had constructive notice. In Ortega v. K-Mart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200, 1203, the court explains that where the source of the dangerous condition is unknown, a failure of the owner to inspect the premises within a reasonable time "is sufficient to allow an inference that the condition was on the floor long enough to give the owner the opportunity to discover and remedy it." Whether defendant's hourly inspection interval policy was adequate is a question for the trier of fact. (Nicholas Dec., 2:21-23). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: CENTRAL VALLEY BUILDERS SUPPLY, A CALIFORNIA VS. ALLIED FRAMERS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action Lis Pendens And Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 8, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT ALLIED FRAMERS, INC.'S Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action Lis Pendens And Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs. The motion to expunge lis pendens off calendar as moot. The motion for attorneys' fees is denied. Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 405.38 states: "The court shall direct that the party prevailing on any motion under this chapter be awarded the reasonable attorney's fees and costs of making or opposing the motion unless the court finds that the other party acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of attorney's fees and costs unjust." Here, Plaintiff has shown substantial justification in the recording of its notice of withdrawal, therefore attorneys' fees are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
